name: Full SMB/NetBIOS Enumeration
description: Enumerates users, shares, groups, password policy, and OS info from Windows/Samba systems via SMB and RPC.

arguments.-a: Do all simple enumeration (users, shares, groups, OS, password policy)

use_case: First-step enumeration against Windows/Samba hosts to gather users, shares, and domain info.
prerequisites: Target with SMB (port 445) or NetBIOS (port 139) open
common_mistakes: Not trying null session vs authenticated

name: List SMB Shares (Null Session)
description: Lists available SMB shares on a target using a null (anonymous) session.
expected_output:
Sharename       Type      Comment
---------       ----      -------
IPC$            IPC       IPC Service
print$          Disk      Printer Drivers
shared          Disk      Public Files
use_case: Quickly check what shares are available without credentials. Look for readable/writable shares.
detections: Share enumeration in SMB audit logs
common_mistakes: Not trying to connect to each share

name: SMB Share Permission Mapping
description: Maps SMB share permissions showing read/write access for each share. Color-coded output.
expected_output:
[+] IP: 192.168.1.10:445	Name: dc01.corp.local
	Disk	Permissions	Comment
	----	-----------	-------
	ADMIN$	NO ACCESS
	C$	NO ACCESS
	Shared	READ, WRITE
use_case: Identify which shares you can read from or write to — writable shares are high-value targets.
detections: SMB access attempts across multiple shares
remediation: Apply least privilege to share permissions
Remove write access from non-admin shares
common_mistakes: Not recursively listing files in readable shares (-R)
Missing writable shares that allow DLL hijacking

name: SNMP Community String Walk
description: Walks the entire SNMP MIB tree using community string 'public'. Can reveal system info, interfaces, processes, and installed software.
expected_output:
SNMPv2-MIB::sysDescr.0 = STRING: Linux server 5.4.0-42-generic
SNMPv2-MIB::sysName.0 = STRING: web-server-01
HOST-RESOURCES-MIB::hrSWRunName.1 = STRING: apache2
use_case: Extract system information, running processes, network interfaces, and installed software via SNMP.
remediation: Restrict SNMP access to management network
common_mistakes: Not extracting specific OIDs for users/processes

name: LDAP User Enumeration
description: Queries Active Directory via LDAP to enumerate all user accounts. Works with anonymous bind or valid credentials.
expected_output:
dn: CN=Administrator,CN=Users,DC=corp,DC=local
sAMAccountName: Administrator

dn: CN=John Smith,OU=Employees,DC=corp,DC=local
sAMAccountName: j.smith
use_case: Enumerate all domain users via LDAP — useful for building username lists for password attacks.
prerequisites: Anonymous bind allowed or valid credentials
remediation: Restrict LDAP access to authorized systems

name: RPC User Enumeration
description: Enumerate domain users via RPC null session. Also supports group enumeration, password policy, and SID lookups.
expected_output:
user:[Administrator] rid:[0x1f4]
user:[Guest] rid:[0x1f5]
user:[krbtgt] rid:[0x1f6]
user:[svc_sql] rid:[0x44f]
use_case: Enumerate users when SMB null sessions are allowed. Get RIDs for SID brute-forcing.
common_mistakes: Not trying other commands (enumdomgroups, getdompwinfo, querydispinfo)

name: Nmap SMB Enumeration Scripts
description: Uses Nmap NSE scripts to enumerate SMB shares, users, and OS information in a single scan.
expected_output:
| smb-enum-shares:
|   ADMIN$ - Remote Admin
|   C$ - Default share
|   SharedDocs - READ/WRITE
| smb-enum-users:
|   administrator, guest, svc_backup
use_case: Quick SMB enumeration using Nmap when you don't have specialized tools available.
detections: Multiple SMB queries from single source
common_mistakes: Not adding --script-args smbusername=user,smbpassword=pass for authenticated enum

name: DNS Zone Transfer
description: Attempts a DNS zone transfer (AXFR) to dump all DNS records for a domain. If successful, reveals all hostnames.
expected_output:
corp.local.		IN	SOA	dc01.corp.local.
corp.local.		IN	NS	dc01.corp.local.
www.corp.local.		IN	A	192.168.1.20
mail.corp.local.	IN	A	192.168.1.25
use_case: If zone transfers are allowed, get a complete map of all DNS records — reveals internal hostnames and IPs.
remediation: Restrict zone transfers to authorized secondary DNS servers only
common_mistakes: Only trying the primary DNS server (try all NS records)
Not trying both TCP and UDP
